meta_prerequisites.notes: correct_positions_by_question in sidecar [1,3,0,2,1,3] confirmed against GIFT answer-line order for all 6 questions — exact match.

q1.note: Correct: 'The sum divided by the count: (3+5+4+4+4) / 5 = 4' (51 chars). Distractors: 42, 45, 63 chars; mean=50.0. 51 < 60.0.

q2.note: Correct: '4, the middle value after sorting all nine scores' (49 chars). Distractors: 52, 61, 45 chars; mean=52.67. 49 < 63.2.

q3.note: Correct: 'Mode, because it counts occurrences and works on text labels' (59 chars). Distractors: 67, 77, 70 chars; mean=71.33. 59 < 85.6.

q4.note: Correct: 'Median, because the middle value is not pulled by outliers' (57 chars). Distractors: 61, 75, 72 chars; mean=69.33. 57 < 83.2.

q5.note: Correct: 'Temperature 0 produces a strong mode; temperature 1 produces a weak or absent mode' (82 chars). Distractors: 77, 90, 82 chars; mean=83.0. 82 < 99.6.

q6.note: Correct: 'A high mean alone does not confirm consistency; the median and mode are also needed' (83 chars). Distractors: 69, 92, 92 chars; mean=84.33. 83 < 101.2.

length_bias.notes: Explanatory clauses removed from correct answers in retry-1. No correct answer exceeds mean_distractor_len * 1.20 in any question. Rate drops from 1.00 to 0.00.

weakest_questions.distractor: Temperature, because adjusting it removes the poor runs from the data set
weakest_questions.note: Wrong and unrelated to common statistical misconceptions, but temperature is a live concept in this week so a beginner plausibly confuses model settings with data filtering. Not absurd.

weakest_questions.distractor: The mean of 88% is probably a calculation error; the median should always be calculated first
weakest_questions.note: Oddly framed but not absurd; a beginner might conflate calculation precedence with analytical priority.

beginner_fairness_and_scope.notes: All stems use plain language defined in corpus. No advanced statistics required. All 6 questions answerable by a learner who read only this topic corpus. Temperature distractors reference concepts introduced in corpus §5.5 and topic prerequisites.

verdict: Pass — length-bias fix successful (rate 1.00 -> 0.00); all five checks clean; all LOs covered; no positional, plausibility, or scope failures.
